Football.title: Liverpool plot stunning Jarrell Quansah transfer return thanks to secret £70m clause one year after £30m Leverkusen move
Football.content:
LIVERPOOL are plotting a sensational return for Jarell Quansah just one year after his £30million move to Bayer Leverkusen.

The Reds inserted a secret buy‑back clause worth a staggering €80million, around £70m, when Quansah left Anfield last summer.





That clause, sources say, remains valid until the end of May, giving Arne Slot’s side a narrow window to bring the defender back to Merseyside.

Quansah, 23, has been a revelation in the Bundesliga with Leverkusen, impressing with his commanding presence at the back.

His performances have caught the eye across Europe, but it’s Liverpool who may benefit most from his rapid development.

The centre‑half was a fans’ favourite at Anfield and was tipped for a long future in red before opting for regular first‑team football in Germany.



Now, with injuries and inconsistencies at the heart of Liverpool’s defence this season, the club’s hierarchy are said to be contemplating activating the clause.

That would see Quansah return just 12 months after leaving, a stunning turn of events in modern transfer dealings.

It’s understood talks off the pitch are already underway, with Liverpool bracing themselves to move fast if they decide to trigger the release.

Quansah’s ability to play out from the back and his strong aerial game would address key areas of concern for Slot.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

The Dutch boss, however, must weigh up financial realities and squad balance before committing to such a hefty outlay.

Despite the lure of bringing a cult hero back, there are doubts over whether Leverkusen will make the process anything but straightforward.

Meanwhile, uncertainty continues to swirl around defender Ibrahima Konate at Liverpool.

The France international’s contract talks are currently in limbo, leaving his future at Anfield very much up in the air with Real Madrid strongly linked.



Other clubs across Europe are reportedly monitoring the situation, sensing an opportunity should negotiations collapse or stall further.

To add to Liverpool’s defensive woes, French centre‑back Jeremy Jacquet could be ruled out for the rest of the season after undergoing surgery.

The £60million star only agreed to join the Reds from Stade Rennais in January, with Liverpool beating Chelsea to his signature after a fierce transfer battle.

Now, with Jacquet sidelined and Konate’s future uncertain, the lure of bringing Quansah back looks even more compelling for Klopp and co.

Football.title: Hollywood star Sydney Sweeney shows off her ball skills and names ex-Man Utd ace as favourite player
Football.content:
SYDNEY SWEENEY has shown off her football skills during an appearance in Portugal.

The Hollywood star went viral after taking to the pitch after Sporting Lisbon‘s 3-0 win over Estoril.





Sweeney, 28, could be seen dribbling with the ball and enjoying herself at the Estadio Jose Alvalade.

She played with the club’s mascot and did her best attempt at some keepy-ups.

During her visit to the European country, she also made the bold revelation of her favourite footballer.

The White Lotus star revealed that former Manchester United star Cristiano Ronaldo would have been an inspiration to her.



She said: “Cristiano Ronaldo is my favourite footballer.

“I’m a competitive person, and if I were a footballer, he would be my inspiration.”

The comment came as she was at the home of the five-time Balon d’Or winner.

Ronaldo came through the ranks at Sporting Lisbon before he made the move to Old Trafford in 2003.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

During the actress’ time at the iconic stadium, she was given the VIP treatment.

Sweeney was presented with her Sporting Lisbon kit by club official Andre Bernado.

The shirt had ‘Sydney’ printed on the back as she wore a cap, hoodie, leather jacket and jeans.

She watched the action from inside the directors’ box before making her way onto the turf.

During the kickabout, she scored a goal and bizarrely copied the covering-ears celebration that is usually performed by current Red Devil Bruno Fernandes.



Ronaldo, 41, is currently playing his football in Saudi Arabia with Al-Nassr, having left United after a second spell in 2022.

He is chasing his first Saudi Pro League title since arriving in 2023, with the club top of the league by two points.

Between his two spells at United, Ronaldo played for both Real Madrid and Juventus.

Since joining Al-Nassr, he has scored 115 goals in 131 appearances in all competitions.

Football.title: Jurgen Klopp ‘could be set for Red Bull exit’ with Premier League manager tipped to replace Liverpool legend
Football.content:
JUR OUT? Jurgen Klopp ‘could be set for Red Bull exit’ with Premier League manager tipped to replace Liverpool legend

JURGEN KLOPP is reportedly in danger of quitting his role at Red Bull after “tensions” with company chiefs.

The former Liverpool boss, 58, was only appointed as Red Bull’s Global Head of Football last January.





It came after he ended his nine-year reign at Anfield, with the German claiming he needed a break from top-level management.

Instead, Klopp took charge of a network of clubs including RB Leipzig, Salzburg and New York Red Bulls — but there is now said to be “friction” with top executives.

But according to Australian newspaper Salzburger Nachrichten, there is now friction between Klopp and Red Bull’s top executives.

The reported rift is said to be linked to the performance of Red Bull’s clubs, particularly Leipzig.



Leipzig are fifth in the Bundesliga table and 19 points adrift of league leaders Bayern Munich.

But Red Bull poured cold water on the claims as managing director Oliver Mintzlaff hit back: “It’s completely absurd and unfounded. We are satisfied with Jurgen Klopp’s work.”

However, L’Equipe claim Klopp’s appointment of Ole Werner as Leipzig boss last summer has drawn “considerable criticism”.

The Premier League legend was involved in firing Salzburg boss Thomas Letsch and Paris FC coach Stephane Gilli in recent weeks.



But it is claimed Klopp was frozen out of selecting the new manager for Paris FC, who Red Bull acquired in October 2024.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

The French side hired Antoine Kombouare, despite Klopp reportedly preferring former Red Bull coaches Marco Rose or Adi Hutter.

Klopp is said to be “not particularly happy” and the prospect of a summer exit is reportedly growing.

The report even suggests the Champions League winner is considering a shock return to the dugout —



Crystal Palace boss Oliver Glasner is a candidate to replace him in the role at the end of the season, the report says.

Austrian coach Glasner has already confirmed he will leave Palace when his contract expires in June.

Klopp is still considered as one of the best coaches in the game after he guided Liverpool to a Premier League and Champions League title.

He also lifted the FA Cup and two Carabao Cups during his stint on Merseyside.

Football.title: Middlesbrough star in embarrassing wardrobe malfunction during Birmingham clash as fans say ‘am I going mad?’
Football.content:
FANS were left baffled after spotting Middlesbrough forward David Strelec wearing a different shirt during the club’s 3-1 win over Birmingham.

The 24-year-old scored once as Boro clinched a crucial victory at St. Andrew’s.





But while celebrating his goal, eagle-eyed viewers noticed that his shirt was different.

Rather than having a Unibet sponsor like his team-mates, Strelec had a Utilita-branded strip.

Some fans have suggested it was the same shirt he wore while playing with the under-21s on his return from injury.

Others believe it was a kitman error at half-time as he was spotted wearing the standard shirt in the first half, and may have picked up the wrong shirt during the interval.



One person took to social media to write: “The way football’s going, wouldn’t be surprised if we see individual players having individual shirt sponsors.

“Just didn’t think Boro’s David Strelec would be the guinea pig.”

Another joked: “Can we keep Strelec in the Utilita shirt.”

A third said: “Strelec wearing an U21 shirt?”



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

While a fourth commented: “Am I going mad, or did Strelec have a different shirt on to the rest of the Middlesbrough team?”

Middlesbrough won 3-1 against Birmingham thanks to a brace from Matt Targett as well as Strelec’s goal.

It left them second in the Championship table, just five points behind league leaders Coventry.

Football.title: Declan Rice injury update drops as Mikel Arteta reveals Arsenal latest after scare
Football.content:
Declan Rice injury update drops as Mikel Arteta reveals Arsenal latest after scare

Arsenal star Declan Rice was taken off against Chelsea, and now Mikel Arteta has delivered a new injury update ahead of the Gunners' Premier League clash with Brighton & Hove Albion

View 2 Images Declan Rice came off for Arsenal against Chelsea (Image: Shaun Botterill/Getty Images)

Mikel Arteta has provided an Arsenal injury update on Declan Rice ahead of their Premier League trip to Brighton.

‌



The Gunners manager revealed the midfielder requested to be taken off late in Sunday's victory over Chelsea after seemingly aggravating his knee during a recovery run.

‌



With the swift turnaround to Wednesday's trip to the Amex Stadium, Rice became a significant fitness concern, whilst doubts also surfaced regarding David Raya following the 2-1 triumph at the Emirates Stadium. However, Arteta was able to deliver encouraging news on both players.

‌



When asked if Rice is OK at his Tuesday press conference, Arteta said: "I hope so. He was much better today. Obviously, we haven't really trained because we have only two days to prepare for the game, so hopefully he's going to be better tomorrow. David is good, good to go."

Arsenal were missing Martin Odegaard for their most recent London derby, with the Norwegian captain troubled by fitness concerns in recent weeks.

Arsenal star reveals his big problem with London after hinting he wants to leave READ MORE :

He recovered from a minor knee complaint to face Tottenham last month but was missing from Sunday's matchday squad, with Arteta explaining he was still experiencing discomfort and therefore it was deemed too risky to include him.

‌



Kai Havertz returned to the squad and featured for 15 minutes, with Arteta providing fresh updates on both players. In his latest assessment, he said: "Yeah, especially Kai, he's done a part of the session, so we'll wait and see if he is okay. Martin is the other one, we'll see. We have to wait and see how comfortable he is to do certain actions."

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Arteta subsequently confirmed that Ben White remains sidelined. Regarding other injury updates, Max Dowman continued his rehabilitation from an ankle problem on Friday night, featuring for 45 minutes in an under-21s fixture.

‌



Should Rice be unavailable against the Seagulls, Mikel Merino would have been the ideal stand-in but he is presently sidelined with a foot injury requiring surgery.

View 2 Images Mikel Arteta gave injury updates on several Arsenal players (Image: Getty Images)

One encouraging note for Arsenal was Arteta confirming no new fitness concerns emerged, with fixtures against Brighton, Mansfield Town, Bayer Leverkusen (x2) and Everton on the horizon.

‌



Fabian Hurzeler has an equally limited injury list with just two players definitively ruled out, as the Brighton manager suggested Yasin Ayari could make his return.

Hurzeler said: "He trained yesterday, so hopefully he reacts fine to the first training session, and then we have another one today, and I'm quite confident that he will be an option for tomorrow."

Adam Webster and Stefanos Tzimas are the two long-term casualties for Brighton, with both having undergone knee operations.

Football.title: 'I lived in Iran to play professional football and what I saw left me shocked - it was crazy'
Football.content:
'I lived in Iran to play professional football and what I saw left me shocked - it was crazy'

A footballer has spoken about his experience of playing for an Iranian club

View 2 Images A former La Liga goalkeeper spent part of his career in Iranian football (Image: David Ramos/Getty Images)

Manu Fernandez spent six months playing for an Iranian club and was stunned by his experience. The Spanish goalkeeper soon regretted his decision to move out to the Middle East in 2016.

‌



The 39-year-old, who has now hung up his boots, rose through Sporting Gijon’s academy and reached the heights of La Liga at Deportivo La Coruna. Fernandez had spent his entire career in Spain when he accepted an offer to join Machine Sazi in the Persian Gulf Pro League.

‌



The shot-stopper was unprepared for the cultural differences he encountered during his brief time in the Middle East. Fernandez made a total of 17 appearances for Machine Sazi before securing a return to Spanish football with third-tier side Marbella halfway through the season.

‌



Israel and the USA recently launched strikes against Iran, which resulted in the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and several other Iranian officials over the weekend and prompted retaliatory action. Watching the escalating conflict from afar, Fernandez has reflected on his brief time in the country.

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

"I don't know if the way this is being provoked is the best, with Israel and the United States going all out, but I wish them the best,” Fernandez told Spanish publication Sport. “That they become freer than they are now, that they have a democratic regime and can make their own decisions."

‌



Man Utd boss Michael Carrick gives update on two players for Newcastle clash after injury concern READ MORE :

The former Spain U20 international was taken aback by the contrast between life in his homeland and Iran. Fernandez recalled one of his Machine Sazi team-mates appearing in court after his tattoos were spotted during a game.

On another occasion, the goalkeeper claims a club employee sent him up to his room after he was seen talking to a woman in a hotel lobby, with the divides between genders leaving a lasting impression. Fernandez believes the ongoing conflict could eventually lead to major cultural shifts in Iran.

“Because of everything that's happening, I don't know if they're starting to wake up or if they see that people outside are finally paying attention to them,” Fernandez said. “The regime's way of controlling the population is by limiting access to the outside world.

‌



View 2 Images Fernandez recalled the differences he noticed compared to his life in Spain (Image: MIGUEL RIOPA/AFP via Getty Images)

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

“It's like having a flock of sheep, locked up. And that's very easy. But if people start to learn about what's out there... That's why what's happening surprises me, because you saw so many people devoted to the regime, and at the same time it doesn't surprise me, given that it seems impossible to live like that, with so many restrictions, for so long.”

‌



Fernandez faced obstacles when attempting to cut his time at Machine Sazi short. The goalkeeper claims that he began plotting his escape, following the death of his father, after learning his passport had been retained.

"I took advantage of an opportunity that arose when the football club failed to pay me to sneak out,” Fernandez said. “I packed everything in the early hours of the morning because the flights to Istanbul left at night, and I didn't tell anyone on the team.

“Fortunately, I wasn't stopped at the airport security checks. I wouldn't recommend what I experienced there to anyone.”

Football.title: Man Utd boss Michael Carrick gives update on two players for Newcastle clash after injury concern
Football.content:
Man Utd boss Michael Carrick gives update on two players for Newcastle clash after injury concern

Manchester United manager Michael Carrick is sweating on the fitness of Harry Maguire and Luke Shaw ahead of the Premier League clash with Newcastle

View 2 Images Manchester United defender Luke Shaw came off during Sunday's defeat of Crystal Palace (Image: Molly Darlington/Copa/Getty Images)

Manchester United manager Michael Carrick is set to give Harry Maguire and Luke Shaw additional time to show they are fit for the clash against Newcastle United on Wednesday, after both were had to withdraw during the victory over Crystal Palace due to illness.

‌



Shaw was substituted halfway through the first half, replaced by Noussiar Mazraoui, while Maguire exited during the second half and was replaced by Ayden Heaven. Neither defender sustained an injury, with Carrick confirming both were unwell.

‌



United's interim boss is now optimistic they will recover sufficiently to retain their spots in the starting line-up against Newcastle at St James' Park, with both included in the squad that travelled to Tyneside on Tuesday afternoon.

‌



"We're hoping so. We're working toward it," Carrick told the Manchester Evening News when questioned if Shaw and Maguire would be fit. "They weren't feeling too good, so we've still got a bit of time to the game and we'll just have to see how they feel. We'll obviously give them every chance."

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

"It wasn't an injury. Both of them weren't feeling too good. It's one of them things really, but we'll just have to see how they are today and tomorrow morning."

‌



United might be missing Lisandro Martinez and Mason Mount at Newcastle, with neither yet back in full training. Martinez has been absent for the last two matches with a calf problem, while Mount has missed five games due to an injury he sustained in training.

Matthijs de Ligt hasn't featured for United since the end of November as he continues to manage a back issue. As for Newcastle, meanwhile, they may well have to deal with a number of absences for the clash with striker Nick Woltemade potentially missing out.

View 2 Images Nick Woltemade could miss the game (Image: Serena Taylor/Newcastle United via Getty Images)

Article continues below

"Nick has come down ill," Magpies boss Eddie Howe said. "There's potentially an illness in the squad. He hasn't trained but we will give him every chance for tomorrow.

"Jacob trained fine yesterday. Tino Livramento is getting closer and closer. This game possibly comes too soon for him but we are desperate for him to become available."

Football.title: Arsenal 'made transfer contact with striker' after Mikel Arteta stunned by performance
Football.content:
Arsenal 'made transfer contact with striker' after Mikel Arteta stunned by performance

Arsenal saw off Inter Milan at the start of the year but Mikel Arteta was hugely impressed with their young Italian striker, Pio Esposito, who made a substitute cameo

View 2 Images Mikel Arteta has set his sights on Inter Milan's young striker after seeing him against Arsenal (Image: Catherine Ivill - AMA/Getty Images)

Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta has become a huge admirer of Inter Milan young gun Pio Esposito after seeing him up close in a Champions League clash in January - leading to a call being put in to the Italians.

‌



The Gunners saw off the Serie A giants 3-1 at the San Siro earlier this year, but Arteta came away massively impressed by their 20-year-old forward. Esposito has reportedly ‘remained in the eyes and thoughts’ of Arteta and he asked his sporting director, Andrea Berta, to learn more about him.

‌



Esposito came on in place of Lautaro Martinez in the 63rd minute and impressed during his cameo performance. Such was Esposito’s impact, Arteta even felt the need to send on Gabriel for Cristhian Mosquera, who had endured a difficult time containing the attacker.

‌



La Gazzetta dello Sport reports that Berta ‘quickly contacted’ Inter sporting director Piero Ausilio to see whether Esposito was for sale and at what price. But the Italians hit back with a firm rejection, highlighting that the striker was central to their future plans.

The report adds that Inter made it clear there was ‘no chance’ of a closing a deal for the Italian international. Esposito himself is thought to be very happy at the San Siro and is not even considering the prospect of leaving Inter.

Arsenal star reveals his big problem with London after hinting he wants to leave READ MORE :

Inter boss Christian Chivu previously said on his frontman: "Despite being so young, he handles the pressure well, he can also handle the physical duel on the pitch, and works hard for the team. I’m very pleased with how he is doing." He also added: "The strikers complete each other... Pio can do a bit of everything: I'm happy for him."

‌



Arsenal are not short of firepower having spent big again in the summer, which allowed them to land the likes of Viktor Gyokeres and Eberechi Eze. They added to a forward line that already includes Gabriel Jesus, Bukayo Saka and Kai Havertz.

View 2 Images Pio Esposito made an impression off the bench against the Gunners

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Despite the quality that exists at the Emirates they remain short of an elite goalscorer, the likes of which is possessed by some of their rivals. At present that isn't halting their ambitions and winning the quadruple remains on the cards for the north Londoners this season.

Gyokeres is the only player to hit double figures for the Gunners this term, but more was expected of him in front of goal. Esposito has scored seven times in 36 outings for Inter, with the majority of his opportunities coming from the bench as he works his way up the pecking order.

Football.title: 100-day countdown for 2026 World Cup begins with huge off-the-pitch issues dominating
Football.content:
One hundred days and counting until the biggest World Cup in football history. The expanded 48 team tournament - which kicks off on June 11 - will also be the most controversial, the most expensive and indulgent we have ever seen.

From a football point of view, the tactical trends will be more important than ever, while heat and weather conditions across the United States, Mexico and Canada will play a huge part.

If the Club World Cup is anything to go by, brace yourself for storm delays and travel chaos while armchair viewers in the United Kingdom will probably lose track of what time of day it is.

There are already growing calls for boycotts with US president Donald Trump taking such a central role alongside FIFA president Gianni Infantino.

‌



It is impossible to imagine, as things stand, how Iran could possibly play in a country which is currently bombing its people. That should sit so uncomfortably not just for Iran but other nations.

England players voice concerns as political issues hang over 2026 World Cup READ MORE :

The World Cup is always portrayed as a celebration and yet the world is on the brink. It is way too glib and simplistic to say football will fix that and we will just get on with it.

This is a tournament like no other. Yes, we have had fears in the past - security in South Africa, the controversy of Qatar and it is incredible to think Russia hosted the 2018 World Cup - but hosting it across three countries raises all sorts of debate about the environment.

‌



View 4 Images Thomas Tuchel and Harry Kane will hope to lead England to glory this summer (Image: Getty Images)

VAR expansion 'good for tournament'

When we finally get down to the football, the law changes will be so noticeable. Corners and yellow cards will be added to the scope of VAR.

We will see countdown clocks used to cut down on time-wasting on goal-kicks, throw-ins and substitutions. I cannot see how people think that is a bad thing. It has faced some ridicule because FIFA are effectively “trialling” it at the World Cup.

Who remembers what happened in 2018? That was the first World Cup to use VAR. It went smoothly and video referees in major tournaments have worked well since.

‌



Guess what? The Premier League took another year to bring VAR to English football for the 2019/20 season - and that delay was very much in keeping with the difference between how it works in World Cups and in our own top flight.

View 4 Images Expect even more uses of VAR than seen in the Premier League (Image: Getty Images)

The World Cup has the best officials on the pitch and in the video hubs. They are the pick from each country. And it shows.

That is why, actually, the changes will be good for football. Let’s kick out the time wasters and mistakes. FIFA does it quickly compared to the Premier League’s snail pace.

‌



What would be really bad would be to suddenly foist a change to the offside law at the last minute. Arsene Wenger’s proposal to use the new “daylight” rule will be trialled in Canada.

Again, why would you not embrace something that makes the game more entertaining and a better spectacle with more goals? Especially at a time when we are obsessed with the Premier League becoming a turn-off. It’s just not ready yet. But it’s a good idea.

Tuchel will follow Premier League's set-piece trend

One thing you can be sure of is that England manager Thomas Tuchel will follow the trend of relying on set-pieces.

In the heat of the US, that will be vital. Pumping the ball into the box. Look at the set-piece specialists. Declan Rice, Bukayo Saka and Reece James on corners duty. Then big Dan Burn in the box in the 97th minute to score from a set-piece.

‌



There can be no doubt that set-pieces will be big. But if you actually followed the Club World Cup - even in the heat of New Jersey for the semi finals and final - Chelsea played at an incredibly high tempo and blitzed Paris Saint Germain. They did not see it coming. I also think we will see less high press and teams playing with two strikers.

View 4 Images Declan Rice's corners will be key for England this summer

And the not so good...

What pains me is what the cost means for fans. The fans make any tournament. The colour, the noise and the memories. But so many are being priced out.

It is easy to blame the sky-high ticket prices. But actually American concerts and sporting events do cost that much. They really do. And the travel and the hotels. It will be an expensive World Cup.

‌



FIFA tried to address the issue with cheaper entry level tickets. As time passes and we get closer, it would be fabulous to see more and also see more help with travel and accommodation. If fans stay away in their droves, it will kill the tournament stone dead.

I like the new tennis style draw and seedings. You can see the teams’ path. You can map England’s route to glory.

Will they get there? I think it’s a long shot. But Tuchel gives them a chance. As do set-pieces. Corners and free kicks might be dirty words at the moment - but they can be England’s friend in the summer.

Football.title: Erling Haaland injury latest as Man City boss Pep Guardiola gives update for Nottingham Forest
Football.content:
Erling Haaland injury latest as Man City boss Pep Guardiola gives update for Nottingham Forest

Erling Haaland missed Manchester City's 1-0 win over Leeds United due to a minor injury sustained in training and Pep Guardiola has provided an update ahead of facing Nottingham Forest

View 2 Images Manchester City striker Erling Haaland (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

Erling Haaland is feeling 'much better' but will undergo assessment following Manchester City's training session on Tuesday before a decision is made on his availability for the Nottingham Forest clash. Haaland sat out the Leeds trip with a minor training knock sustained on Thursday, and Pep Guardiola was unable to confirm his fitness prospects immediately after that match.

‌



The Norwegian striker remains doubtful for the fixture, though City are hopeful of his inclusion following the weekend's rest. Similarly, Nico O'Reilly, who hobbled off with an ankle complaint during the 1-0 victory over Leeds, was able to participate in Sunday's recovery session.

‌



"He feels much better but we didn't train yesterday. Now we have training and will decide today," Guardiola said of Haaland in a pre-match press conference on Tuesday.

‌



City dealt well without their talismanic striker, with Antoine Semenyo netting the game's only goal while playing up front alongside Omar Marmoush and Guardiola didn't sound too concerned before kick off on the weekend. "Two days ago in training he had some problems and a little injury. Not a big issue but not ready for today," he said at Elland Road.

FOLLOW OUR MAN CITY FB PAGE! Follow our Manchester City Mirror Football Facebook page

Beyond that, City's injury situation looks encouraging as they pursue silverware this campaign. Jeremy Doku made his return to the matchday squad for the first time in the Premier League encounter at Leeds, whilst Savinho entered as a substitute in the second period for his first outing since sustaining his injury on New Year's Day.

‌



That leaves Mateo Kovacic and Josko Gvardiol as the sole absentees, with Guardiola expressing optimism that Kovacic has resumed training.

"It is a privilege to be in March and we are in all the competitions. It's a dream," Guardiola added. "It is a privilege. Last season, we fought but were out of the Champions League and FA Cup. Next Saturday against Newcastle, two games against Real Madrid. If you win, you continue, if you don't win, holidays in that competition. I am only concerned about that."

View 2 Images Pep Guardiola hopes to have Erling Haaland back (Image: Visionhaus, Visionhaus/Getty Images)

‌



City are aiming to narrow the gap on Arsenal on Wednesday night, with both teams in action simultaneously. City welcome Forest whilst Arsenal journey to Brighton, the team that defeated Forest at the weekend; a five-point difference separates the two sides, although Arsenal have played one match more.

Following Forest, City have two significant journeys to gear up for, with pivotal matches against Newcastle in the FA Cup on Saturday and then the first leg of their Champions League last-16 encounter in Madrid. Haaland has already found the net at the Bernabeu but is yet to score against Newcastle in four attempts this season.

Football.title: Man Utd youngster says Cristiano Ronaldo 'wasn't friendly' before frank sauna discussion
Football.content:
Man Utd youngster says Cristiano Ronaldo 'wasn't friendly' before frank sauna discussion

Former Manchester United defender Willy Kambwala came across Cristiano Ronaldo when he was a teenager, with the pair clashing in training before later making up

View 2 Images Willy Kambwala made an early impression on Cristiano Ronaldo in Manchester

Former Manchester United youngster Willy Kambwala admits his first interaction with Cristiano Ronaldo "wasn't friendly" as the two clashed in training - before the legendary figure praised him in private.

‌



Kambwala, who is now plying his trade in Spain with Villarreal, came through the ranks and debuted in December 2023 against West Ham when Erik ten Hag was in charge. Prior to his first outing the defender found himself mixing it in training with the first team.

‌



As a result he came up against Ronaldo during his second stint in Manchester, but a confrontation saw things get heated. Kambwala believes the forward didn't like his physicality and the defender didn't take well to Ronaldo airing his views with the coaches.

‌



They would later cross paths in the sauna and Ronaldo asked the defender to stick around for a chat. There he made it known how much he admired his physical approach.

Kambwala told Orange Sports: "My first contact with Cristiano wasn’t friendly. I’m a very aggressive person and I like challenges and confrontations.

Michael Carrick told what Sir Alex Ferguson trait he must copy to land Man Utd job READ MORE :

"That bothered him a bit. I pushed him slightly and he got angry. He went to speak to the coach, and I didn’t like that either. After training, we met in the sauna. I got up to leave and he asked me to stay. We talked as if nothing had happened. It was amazing. He told me he admired my intensity and aggression."

‌



Kambwala continued to get opportunities under Ten Hag but eventually moved to La Liga, where he has been far more of a regular. Villarreal's swoop could eventually see them spend £9.6million on the defender, but he's been plagued by injuries throughout this season.

View 2 Images Cristiano Ronaldo was impressed with Willy Kambwala - and told him so (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Ronaldo left his mark on Kambwala but didn't shy away from claiming that the young stars of today have it easier than he did - and he cannot see the same drive to succeed.

"I don't mean that they don't respect the more experienced players or the older players, but they live in a different era," he said on Talk TV. "I can see my kid [who is] 12 years old. The mentality is not the same, they are not suffering. Their hunger [is different]. I think they have things more easily, everything's easy, they don't suffer -- and don't care.

"I don't mean only a few in Manchester United, but all the teams, in all leagues in the world, the youngsters are not the same of my generation."

Football.title: Fin Smith starts as England make nine changes for Italy
Football.content:
Fin Smith will start for England in their Six Nations match against Italy on Saturday as Steve Borthwick makes wholesale changes to his team.

Borthwick has made nine personnel changes in light of back-to-back defeats against Scotland and Ireland, results which leave England out of title contention ahead of their game at Stadio Olimpico in Rome.

Tommy Freeman moves from wing to outside centre while Fin Smith replaces George Ford at fly-half.

Rising star Henry Pollock is dropped back to the bench after making his first start against Ireland.

Image: Fin Smith will start for England against Italy

Jamie George, Alex Coles, Guy Pepper, Ben Spencer, Fin Smith, Cadan Murley, Seb Atkinson and Tom Roebuck all also come into the team.

Maro Itoje will captain the team and No 8 Ben Earl will win his 50th cap for England, having made his debut against Scotland in 2020.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player England's Jamie George says their Six Nations fixture against Italy will be the toughest ever, but he feels the squad are going to be extremely well prepared

"We're expecting a big test in Rome and it's one the players are really looking forward to," said Borthwick.

"We know the challenge Italy will bring at the Stadio Olimpico, and we've selected a team we believe will deliver the level of performance we're striving for."

On Earl's milestone, Borthwick added: "To reach 50 caps for England speaks volumes about Ben's professionalism and the consistency of his performances. He's an important leader within this group, someone who drives standards and brings energy every day. It's a wonderful achievement and one he should be very proud of."

England team to face Italy

Starting XV: 15 Elliot Daly, 14 Tom Roebuck, 13 Tommy Freeman,12 Seb Atkinson, 11 Cadan Murley, 10 Fin Smith, 9 Ben Spencer; 1 Ellis Genge, 2 Jamie George, 3 Joe Heyes, 4 Maro Itoje (c), 5 Alex Coles, 6 Guy Pepper, 7 Tom Curry, 8 Ben Earl.

Replacements: 16 Luke Cowan-Dickie, 17 Bevan Rodd, 18 Trevor Davison, 19 Ollie Chessum, 20 Sam Underhill, 21 Henry Pollock, 22 Jack van Poortvliet, 23 Marcus Smith.

Football.title: ‘No holding back!’ Hamilton still focused on 'dream' for 20th year
Football.content:
Lewis Hamilton has promised "no holding back" as he embarks on his 20th season of Formula 1 "still standing, still hungry, still focused on the dream".

Hamilton prepares for this weekend's season-opening Australian Grand Prix having already said he feels "re-set and refreshed" ahead of a second campaign with Ferrari in which he will bid to quickly banish the disappointments of the first, when he failed to record a podium finish for the first time in his career.

Ferrari head to Melbourne hotly tipped alongside Mercedes to fight at the front of the field after an impressive pre-season of testing and seven-time champion Hamilton has made clear he is looking forward to his landmark campaign.

Writing on Instagram in a post containing images from his record-breaking career so far, the 41-year-old said: "Twenty seasons as an F1 driver. It's hard to even grasp the reality of that number.

"It started with a dream. A dream some called ridiculous and said would never amount to anything. Despite that, the dream never changed, and I never stopped chasing it.

"A dream has to be matched with action and, above all, belief. Belief that doesn't break no matter what's thrown in your face or what you come up against. There will always be people who doubt you, people who try to block you, but you can't ever stop fighting.

Instagram Instagram , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Instagram cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enablecookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Instagram cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Instagram cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

"Twenty years filled with incredible highs and brutal lows, with more mistakes than I can count. But those mistakes made the journey even sweeter. You hold the power to your destiny. Everything you need to succeed is within you. You will need help along the way, like I did, but that spark, that fire, is already inside you.

"I'm forever grateful for the lessons, the quiet moments, the chaos, and the many people who helped me chase and achieve my dreams.

"I'm grateful for the doubters and for the ones who tried to knock me down, too. I'm still here, 20 years on, still standing, still hungry, still focused on the dream.

"No holding back."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports F1's Ted Kravitz looks closer at how F1 in 2026 will look as the sport starts a new era of recharge mode, more electric power, new teams and engine suppliers

Can Hamilton enjoy a stronger 2026 campaign?

In a feature assessing the grid's leading driver line-ups, Sky Sports F1's Anthony Davidson said:

"Lewis has found things tough the last couple of years. Let's see if he can bounce back with this new iteration of cars.

"I believe they're going to suit his style more. He'll be able to get more out of the equipment than he has done in the last couple of years with the ground-effects cars.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Former racing driver Anthony Davidson believes if Hamilton can manage qualifying, he could compete for his eighth world title

"[Team-mate Charles] Leclerc is a phenomenal driver, super-fast, probably one of, if not the fastest drivers on the grid, especially in qualifying. He doesn't mind a car that moves around under him. He's very flamboyant in the way he drives, and I really enjoy watching that.

"But I think the pair of them last year at certain points found things tough. I'm sure the car wasn't easy to drive, so let's see how they go this year. I'm expecting them to be a force to be reckoned with once again."

Sky Sports F1's Martin Brundle:

"As F1 fans, we always want Ferrari to do well. I'd love to see that come right for Lewis. He seems a bit happier. He will be great at using the tools available to him on these new cars.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Craig Slater and Martin Brundle review and rank all the F1 teams after pre-season testing

"He's got to beat Charles Leclerc, who is right on it as well.

"A happy Lewis has always been, and not just at Ferrari, a fast Lewis. And I have to say Ferrari have been quite innovative over the winter, so I'm confident Lewis will have a better year."

Football.title: Ryan & Rob to commentate for Wrexham game live on Sky!
Football.content:
Sky Sports has teamed up with Wrexham AFC co-chairmen Rob Mac and Ryan Reynolds, with the pair set to provide live commentary as part of a first-of-its kind broadcast for the Wrexham vs Swansea City EFL Championship fixture on Friday March 13.

Live from Wrexham with Rob & Ryan will be hosted by David Prutton and offers fans a unique matchday experience via commentary and candid conversations from the duo.

Produced by Sky Sports and airing exclusively on Sky Sports Football, fans will also be treated to a host of high-profile guests joining the coverage throughout the match.

Dual broadcasts live on Sky! Rob and Ryan will be providing alternative commentary and you can tune in to that on a separate channel to the regular Sky Sports broadcast, with commentary from Daniel Mann and Andy Hinchcliffe.

"As with our decision to take over Wrexham five years ago, we genuinely have no idea how this is going to go, but we will give it our best. Neither of us have called a sporting event of any variety, let alone a sport we basically learned the rules of five years ago," said the pair.

"Either way, it will be another unpredictable day at the Racecourse and we can't wait. We're grateful to our partners at the EFL and Sky Sports, the latter of which we hope is ready with the bleep button."

Sky Sports presenter David Prutton added: "Rob and Ryan have done an incredible job at Wrexham, and they have really bought into what makes football and the EFL so special.

"We're looking forward to welcoming the guys into our coverage, bringing a unique and entertaining experience for fans and offering something we've not seen before."

Fans can watch Live from Wrexham with Rob & Ryan alongside the main Wrexham vs Swansea match coverage from 7pm on March 13 on Sky Sports Football.

It is an exciting time for Wrexham. They have won three straight promotions - something no club in English football history had ever done - and are in contention for a fourth. They currently sit sixth in the Championship and look on course for the play-offs.

Football.title: Lord Allen resigns as BHA chair after six months in role
Football.content:
The British Horseracing Authority has announced that Lord Allen has resigned as the ruling body’s chair after less than a year in the post.

Lord Charles Allen was nominated for the position late in 2024 after a search and he officially took up the role last September.

In a statement confirming his resignation, Lord Allen said: "Horseracing is an amazing sport with great potential and over the last year I have met some incredibly passionate people who love the sport and who believe to survive and prosper that change is needed.

"I also want to pay tribute to the hardworking and dedicated BHA team. I wish the sport well for the future."

Lord Allen took the role after the member organisations of the BHA (Racecourse Association, Racehorse Owners Association, Thoroughbred Breeders' Association and licensed personnel) unanimously agreed that there should be a completely independent board and that the BHA should have a commercial remit.

The members, who currently nominate representatives to the board, have not been able to reach agreement between themselves on changing the rules of the BHA to allow the achievement of those two goals as a change requires unanimous support. Accordingly, Lord Allen has resigned.

David Jones, the senior independent director of the BHA, said: "The board would like to thank Charles for the time and effort that he has put into the role helping us to develop a vision that is aspirational and achievable, and we regret that we were unable to put in place at this time the governance changes that would have enabled us to progress this. I have enjoyed working with him and wish him continued success."

The BHA board will discuss and determine what interim arrangements will be put in place.

Football.title: Brook: England ready for 'pressure' India semi-final after 'niggly' wins
Football.content:
Captain Harry Brook feels England coming through "niggly" games earlier in the T20 World Cup will stand them in good stead for their blockbuster semi-final against host nation India in Mumbai on Thursday.

Captivate Captivate , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Captivate cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enablecookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Captivate cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Captivate cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

Brook's side stuttered to wins over Associate nations Nepal, Scotland and Italy in the first round around a meek defeat to fellow two-time champions West Indies.

They improved in the Super 8s while going undefeated, beating Sri Lanka, Pakistan - as the skipper struck a stunning 50-ball century - and then finally New Zealand having looked up against it needing 43 from the final 18 deliveries.

England's MVP of the tournament Will Jacks - who has won four Player of the Match awards - and T20 World Cup debutant Rehan Ahmed slammed an unbroken, match-winning stand of 44 from 16 balls against the Black Caps after opening pair Jos Buttler and Phil Salt struggled again, scoring a duck and two respectively.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Full highlights from Colombo as Will Jacks and Rehan Ahmed powered England to a four-wicket win over New Zealand in the T20 World Cup

I always look at T20 cricket as thinking about the reward rather than the risk. It’s a case of backing yourself and thinking about what good can come of it rather than what bad can come of it. I will never tell anyone off for taking the aggressive option – I don’t think I have a leg to stand on there!

Brook told Sky Sports Cricket's Ian Ward: "We are managing to scrape along in close games and that is the sign of a good side.

"I have learned you are never out of games. I know I have players with a lot of character, a willingness to win, wanting to win a lot, but also that calmness under pressure."

'If one of our batters comes off, the opposition are scratching their heads'

Buttler is averaging just 8.85 in the competition after five successive single-figure scores, while Salt's only knock of note was 62 in the Super 8s encounter with Sri Lanka.

However, Brook feels his top-order duo can fire versus India, a side England are meeting for the third straight T20 World Cup semi-final, after hammering them in Adelaide in 2022 before being thrashed by them in Guyana in 2024.

Image: Jos Buttler's scores during his T20 World Cup campaign so far

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Former England all-rounder Moeen Ali is backing Buttler to rediscover best form after yet another disappointing innings, against New Zealand

The Yorkshireman added: "We are all really excited. I have already heard a few lads say they wanted to play India anyway. It is going to be a massive occasion with a hell of a lot of pressure for both sides.

"India obviously have a hell of a lot of support and I'm not sure there are going to be many people behind us. It's going to be loud. Hopefully we can play our best cricket.

"I am excited for the lads up top. I feel they are one step closer to taking the game away from a side. I don't think there is any weakness in the top order at all. The lads have just struggled a little bit and not got the runs they always get.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Take a look at the best bits from Brook's 50-ball century against Pakistan at the T20 World Cup, a knock that featured 10 fours and four sixes

"I think Jos should be left alone. He is one of the best players to have ever played the game and I can see him coming good.

"It's awesome other lads have been able to step up. There have been performances throughout. We have so many powerful players in the top seven that if one comes off, the opposition are scratching their heads and don't really know where to go."

Jacks crucial for England after 'thanks-for-coming game'

Jacks has been instrumental for England, contributing 191 runs at No 7, including across the nerve-jangling wins over Nepal, Italy, Sri Lanka and New Zealand, as well as seven wickets with his off-spin.

Brook said of the Surrey all-rounder: "Jacks has been outstanding. To do that in his first proper World Cup playing every game is phenomenal.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player The best of Jacks' maiden T20 international fifty, an innings that rescued England against Italy and helped the team into the World Cup Super 8s

"We chatted to him in Sri Lanka [during the pre-World Cup tour] when he had a thanks-for-coming game. He didn't bowl, didn't bat and got the ball once or twice in the field.

"We said to him it was a role that gives you a lot of reward when you do win games and that you are playing this role or it is hard for you to get in the side.

"He has embraced that position and done exceptionally well. The character he has shown has been awesome."

'I want to make it as uncomfortable as possible for the batter coming in at all times'

Brook's ice-cool captaincy has also been a key component of England reaching their fifth straight T20 World Cup semi-final, so how has he tried to operate?

"We have had a lot of pressure games and the calmer you can stay as a captain helps in every facet of the game.

"You can see me holding on to the ball a bit longer. I want to have a conversation with the bowler, make sure they know what they are trying to bowl, make them think about one thing.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Brook gives Sky Sports Cricket's Ian Ward an insight into how he leads as a captain

"For example, with wide yorkers, I am not that bothered about the line you bowl. I just want you to hit the yorker length.

"Unless the batter is scooping, no one is going to hit you for four or six. You can set funky fields but if you nail your length it's usually one, dot or wicket.

"With us bowling a lot more spin now, we do try to get ahead of the over rate and slow the game down. It works better in certain situations than others but that is the beauty of playing spin. You can use the maximum amount of minutes you have got.

"I aways have options in my head. I tell the lads who is potentially going to be bowling the second over. Sometimes it changes, sometimes it doesn't. After that it's watch the game, see how it's playing out and try to get the most effective bowler in a situation.

"I want to make it as uncomfortable as possible for the batter coming in at all times."

Watch England vs India in the T20 World Cup semi-finals, at Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai, live on Sky Sports Cricket and Sky Sports Main Event from 1pm on Thursday (1.30pm first ball).

Football.title: Iran’s women’s team decline to sing national anthem before Asian Cup tie
Football.content:
Iran’s women’s football team declined to sing their national anthem ahead of their opening match of the Asian Cup in Australia on Monday, their first fixture since the war in the Middle East began.

Every member of the team stood silently, facing straight ahead, during the anthem prior to kick-off in their Group A match against South Korea, who went on to win 3-0 at the Gold Coast Stadium in Queensland. Iran’s head coach, Marziyeh Jafari, and her players declined to comment on either the war or the death of their long-serving leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, when asked by the media.

The Iranian team arrived in Australia to prepare for the tournament several days before air strikes began in the Middle East. They will face the host nation on Thursday, before playing their third and final group-stage match against the Philippines on Sunday, both at the same venue.

Iran’s players have been praised for their courage. “Our heart goes out to them and their families, it’s a difficult situation and it’s really brave of them to be able to be here and to perform,” said the Australia midfielder Amy Sayer. “They put on a really strong performance, even with the political climate that’s going on and the struggles that they might be going through.

“The best we can do to contribute is to just give them the best game of football [on Thursday] that we’re able and to show them the respect on the field. Hopefully the situation improves and they can keep staying safe in Australia.”

Iran are the only team from the Middle East to have qualified for the tournament, which runs until 21 March. Australia opened their Asian Cup campaign with a 1-0 victory against the Philippines in Perth on Sunday. In Group B, North Korea were 3-0 victors over Uzbekistan.

Football.title: Jennifer Echegini: ‘Winning the Wafcon is on another level. The pride I felt’
Football.content:
Being an integral figure in the distinguished history of Nigeria’s women’s team is an experience that will never dim in the mind of Jennifer Onyinyechi Echegini. Seven months on from beating the hosts Morocco in a pulsating Women’s Africa Cup of Nations final at Rabat’s Olympic Stadium, in the process winning a record 10th African title, “Joe”, as her Paris Saint-Germain teammates call Echegini – an acronym of her three initials – is yet to come down from her career high.

“Winning the Wafcon is on another level, you know?” the 24-year-old midfielder says from Paris. “The pride and the achievement that I felt … when you’re playing with a group of girls that you love and care for, it makes it even more special.”

Overcoming a 2-0 first-half deficit to win 3-2 took every ounce of skill, resilience and fortitude, as Echegini vividly recalls. “We kind of just went [into the second half] with the mindset that it’s all or nothing … you either give everything and maybe get something out of it, or you don’t fight, and you then look back, wishing you had. We just went out with that mindset and gave everything.”

It was the welcome home in Nigeria and the subsequent reception at Aso Rock in Abuja, the seat of Nigeria’s government, where the players were each promised $100,000 (£75,000) and a house for the win (a pledge the government is yet to redeem) that left Echegini truly overwhelmed. “I already knew Nigerians do things big, but when you experience it, it’s on another level,” she says. “Being someone who grew [up] outside Nigeria and not so much into the Nigerian culture, it’s always beautiful to see how things get done. I feel like I’m learning something every time I go home.

“It just shows, as a nation, how supportive everybody is. Without them, winning the tournament wouldn’t have been as special. It was amazing to celebrate things with the people you care about and with fans who care about you and support you.”

Born in the Dutch city of Nijmegen, Echegini moved to England at the age of 11 with her parents before relocating to the US, where she played college football at Mississippi State and Florida State universities. Echegini’s peripatetic life has played a decisive role in the person she has become. “It’s only affected me for the better,” she says. “I would not be who I am or where I am today if I hadn’t experienced such things. I’m very grateful for the moments I’ve had. It’s not the same as an average person who grows up in their home town and goes to their local university. I am very grateful for those experiences.”

View image in fullscreen Jennifer Echegini is known as ‘Joe’ by her Paris Saint-Germain teammates. Photograph: Gualter Fatia/Uefa/Getty Images

Before Randy Waldrum, the American who coached Nigeria at the last World Cup in Australia, gave Echegini her debut in 2022, playing for the Netherlands was what she regarded as a more “realistic” ambition. “I had no contact with Nigeria at all, knew little about it,” she told the EaglesTracker Podcast. “I lived in the Netherlands until I was 11 and playing for the Netherlands seemed more realistic.”

But there has been no looking back ever since Echegini scored her first goal for Nigeria in a 3-0 win over New Zealand in 2023, the same year in which she went to the World Cup in Australia, where Nigeria had an impressive outing, losing to eventual finalists England in the last 16.

To maintain Nigeria’s impressive streak of qualifying for every World Cup since it began in 1991, the Falcons will have to make the last four of this year’s Wafcon, which for a third successive occasion will be held in Morocco, beginning on 17 March. Echegini knows the holders cannot be complacent when defending their title, with the gap between Nigeria and other teams on the continent, once a huge gulf, continuing to narrow.

Echegini also believes Nigeria’s Wafcon preparations are in desperate need of an organisational upgrade. “Preparation time is very limited,” she says. “You have to be very careful with how well you prepare for things. Last year we didn’t prepare as much as we wanted, but we still won. We are a team, regardless of the obstacles thrown at us or the lack of preparation, which always prevails in the end.”

In regards to becoming contenders at next year’s World Cup, should Nigeria qualify as expected, Echegini sounds a note of warning. “We need to prepare very well. We need to figure some things out, so we can be better,” she says. “When you compete with teams that have all these facilities and, essentially, their lives are easier, you’re already at a disadvantage.

“I’m not somebody who wants to complain, because, at the end of the day, football is football. But you have to put your team in the best position to compete. I hope we are able to utilise every camp window, so we can be as strong as we can”. The Nigeria Football Federation would be wise to heed the counsel of one of their national team’s most impressive figures.
